name: Nectar Cakelettes
recipeInstructions:
1. Plump the raisins by steaming them.
2. Chop the plumped raisins.
3. Cream the sugar with shortening until smooth.
4. Add the beaten egg and milk to the mixture, stirring until combined.
5. Mix in the chopped raisins and lemon flavoring.
6. Sift together the flour, baking powder, and salt; add this dry mixture to the wet ingredients, mixing thoroughly.
7. Fill prepared muffin tins about 2/3 full with batter.
8. Bake at a moderate temperature (350°F) for 15 minutes.
9. Allow cakes to cool before scooping out the centers and filling them with raisin custard.
Raisin Custard:
1. Scald 1 cup milk in a saucepan.
2. In a bowl, whisk together 1/4 tsp cornstarch, 1/2 cup raisins (plumped by steaming), 1/2 tsp lemon flavoring, and crumbs from the cake.
3. Whisk 2 egg yolks with 1/4 cup sugar until well combined.
4. Gradually add the yolk mixture to the scalded milk while whisking continuously.
5. Stir in the raisins, lemon flavoring, and crumb mixture.
6. Pour into a shallow dish and set aside.
7. In a clean bowl, beat 2 egg whites with 2 tablespoons sugar until stiff peaks form.
8. Spread the custard into each cooled cake cavity.
9. Bake at a slow temperature (325°F) until a delicate brown forms on top.
